items.label: storefront-dev
messages: Now let me explore the existing codebase structure and reference files.
Wait, there are already directories! Let me check what's in them.
Files already exist! Let me read their current content to see if this is a partial implementation or complete.
Good, the components are already implemented. Now let me check the test files.
Good - the implementation is already in place. Let me verify it works by running the tests.
All 26 tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server can resolve all modules correctly (the mandatory pre-commit gate).
Dev server returned HTTP 200 - all modules resolve correctly. Let me also check the SSR safety checklist and the validation hook.
All checks pass. The feature doesn't add new routes (it's a modal on existing PLP pages), so no new validation hook checks are needed. Let me now check for any uncommitted changes and commit.

No tracked file changes — the implementation files are already committed. The only untracked files are flight data/summary/terminal-log files which are pipeline artifacts. Let me verify the component files are actually tracked in git:

The implementation is fully complete and already committed. All verifications pass:  - ✅ 26/26 unit tests passing - ✅ Dev server HTTP 200 (webpack resolves all modules) - ✅ SSR safety checks clean - ✅ No banned patterns (networkidle, window/document access)  Now let me complete the pipeline.

The **storefront-dev** phase for **product-quick-view** is complete. Here's a summary:  ## Completed Work  ### Implementation (already committed in `d012578`) | File | Description | |---|---| | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` | ProductTile override: wraps base tile with `role="group"` container, adds full-width semi-transparent Quick View overlay bar with slide-up animation on desktop hover, always-visible on mobile | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` | QuickViewModal: Chakra Modal using `useProductViewModal` hook to fetch full product data, renders `ProductView` with spinner/error/content states | | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` | 12 unit tests covering overlay bar rendering, interaction (preventDefault/stopPropagation), set/bundle exclusion, prop forwarding | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` | 14 unit tests covering modal shell, content rendering, error state, accessibility (aria-label, Escape key) |  ### Verification Results - **26/26 unit tests** passing - **Dev server HTTP 200** — all webpack module resolution correct - **SSR safety** — no `window`/`document` access, no hydration mismatch risks - **Accessibility** — semantic `<button>`, `aria-label` with product name, keyboard focus reveals bar, Escape closes modal - **Corner cases** — sets/bundles excluded, missing productId guarded, unavailable product shows error state

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && npm start & ↵ SERVER_PID=$! ↵ echo "Server PID: $SERVER_PID" ↵ sleep 80 ↵ HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000

# SSR Safety checks ↵ echo "=== Checking for window/document access ===" ↵ grep -rn 'window\.' /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx /workspaces

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git log --oneline -5 && echo "---" && git log --oneline --all -- 'apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx' 'apps/commerce-storefront/overrid
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view storefront-dev 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark storefront-dev complete for product-quick-view" 2>&1

messages: Now let me check what source files were created by the previous phase and understand the test infrastructure.
Good - the source files and placeholder test files exist. Let me read the source implementations and existing test files.
The test files already exist with comprehensive tests. Let me run them to see if they pass.
All 26 tests pass across both test suites. Let me verify there are no other tests in the project that might be broken by the new components.
There's a failed test suite. Let me see which one failed.

The only failure is the Playwright e2e test suite (`e2e/storefront-smoke.spec.ts`) which fails because of a Playwright import error - this is a pre-existing infrastructure issue, not related to our unit tests. All 26 unit tests pass with 100% statement/function/line coverage.  Let me check the branch coverage gap (88.88% overall, line 28 uncovered in product-tile). Let me look at what that branch is.
The branch at line 28 has 75% coverage - the `productName` fallback chain (`product?.productName || product?.name || ''`). Let me add tests to cover the remaining branches to improve coverage.
Now let me run the tests again to verify everything passes with the new test cases:
